description:
Write a Python program using the ZMQ Remote API to simulate three spherical ROV hull configurations underwater for 10 seconds each, starting at Z=50.0m. Apply custom buoyancy and drag forces at every simulation step based on the following specifications:

Constants:
- Fluid density (rho) = 1000 kg/m³
- Gravity (g) = 9.81 m/s²
- Drag coefficient (Cd) = 0.47
- Buoyancy Force = rho * V * g (directed upwards, +Z axis)
- Drag Force = -0.5 * rho * Cd * A * v_z * |v_z|

Configurations:
- Config A: Radius = 0.25 m, Mass = 100 kg
- Config B: Radius = 0.25 m, Mass = 150 kg
- Config C: Radius = 0.30 m, Mass = 100 kg

Export the aggregated time-series data to /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/hydrodynamics.csv including columns: config_id, time_s, z_pos_m, z_vel_ms, applied_force_z_n. Record the terminal velocity (steady-state velocity at the end of the 10s run) for each configuration in a report at /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/hydrodynamics_report.json with fields: total_configs, terminal_velocity_A, terminal_velocity_B, terminal_velocity_C.

Login: username=ga, password=password123.
